test.question: If 5 pens cost $2.50, what is the cost of 8 pens at the same rate?
A car travels 180 miles in 3 hours. What is its average speed?
The mean of five numbers is 12. If four of the numbers are 10, 14, 13, and 11, what is the fifth number?
A shirt originally costs $40. If it is discounted by 25%, what is the new price?
If 3 notebooks cost $6.75, how much does 1 notebook cost?
In a class of 30 students, 18 are boys. What percent are girls?
If a train moves at 45 mph, how long will it take to travel 270 miles?
A recipe uses 2 cups of flour for every 3 cups of sugar. If you use 12 cups of sugar, how many cups of flour do you need?
The price of a book increased from $12 to $15. What is the percent increase?
A student scores 80, 85, 78, and 92 on four tests. What is the average?
If 20% of x is 30, what is x?
If 2 pencils cost $0.90, how many pencils can be bought with $2.70?
A car travels 150 miles using 5 gallons of gas. How many miles per gallon does it get?
The median of 2, 4, 6, 8, 10 is:
If 40% of a number is 24, what is the number?
A pair of shoes is originally priced at $80. It is on sale for 20% off. What is the sale price?
A student scored 72 out of 90. What percent did the student score?
If a population increases by 10% every year, what will be the population after one year if the current population is 5000?
What is the average of the first five prime numbers?
If a train travels 300 miles in 5 hours, what is the average speed?
A jar contains 5 red, 3 blue, and 2 green marbles. What is the probability of picking a red marble?
If 3 shirts cost $24, what is the cost of 7 shirts?
A bag contains 6 apples and 4 oranges. What is the ratio of oranges to total fruit?
A class has 12 boys and 18 girls. What is the ratio of girls to boys?
If 15% of a number is 24, what is the number?
A store’s revenue increased from $1,200 to $1,560 over one month. What was the percent increase?
If 60% of a class are boys and there are 24 boys, how many students are in the class?
If a car travels x miles using y gallons of fuel, which expression represents the number of miles per gallon?
A number is increased by 20% and then decreased by 20%. What is the net change?
If a group of workers can complete a job in 8 hours, how long will it take twice as many workers to complete the job, assuming equal productivity?
In a survey, 40% liked tea, 50% liked coffee, and 20% liked both. What percent liked neither?
A student’s grades are 88, 92, 85, and 91. What score must the student get on the next test to average 90?
The average of 8 numbers is 12. If one number is removed and the new average is 11.5, what number was removed?
If 4x = 3y and y = 12, what is x?
A machine produces 120 units in 8 hours. At this rate, how many units are produced in 15 hours?
What is the median of the data set: 12, 18, 25, 27, 30, 35, 42?
The probability of rain on Monday is 0.3 and on Tuesday is 0.5. What is the probability it will rain on both days?
The average of 6 numbers is 10. One more number is added, making the new average 11. What is the number added?
If the exchange rate is 1 USD = 0.85 Euro, how many Euros is $200?
A car’s value depreciates 20% each year. If the car is worth $10,000 now, what is it worth next year?
If a cube has a volume of 64 cubic inches, what is the length of one side?
A box contains 5 red, 3 green, and 2 yellow balls. What is the probability of picking a green ball?
If a car goes 240 miles on 8 gallons of fuel, how many gallons will it need for 360 miles?
A worker earns $15/hour for the first 40 hours and $22.50/hour for overtime. What is the total pay for 50 hours?
What is the range of the data set: 3, 5, 8, 12, 14, 19?
The population of a town increased by 10% annually. If the population is 10,000, what will it be after two years?
A car depreciates by 15% each year. If it is worth $20,000 now, what will it be worth after one year?
If 5 machines can produce 100 units in 4 hours, how many machines are needed to produce 300 units in 4 hours?
If the average of four numbers is 15, and three of them are 12, 18, and 16, what is the fourth number?
A circle has a circumference of 31.4 cm. What is the radius? (Use π = 3.14)
